methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Poseidon Aerospace is a Seed-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $12M+. The valuation exercise considers a universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace. Given the early stage and limited data availability, only one valuation method proved applicable.

Stage-progression anchoring is the primary method, applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $12M total raised proxy, yielding $30M. Peer-median analysis was not feasible due to insufficient narrow-filter matches within Space & Aerospace at the same or adjacent stage; while 23 same-sector peers exist, none of the stage and tier filters produced the three or more datapoints needed for reliable median estimation. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 59.8x across the 6 public tickers) could not be applied because the target's revenue is not on file—standard for private companies.

The triangulated range spans $26M to $34M, centered on $30M. This range reflects the mechanics of the stage-progression method with modest variance around the point estimate. The primary uncertainty is the robustness of the 2.5x multiplier absent a larger matched-peer dataset; validation would benefit from additional Seed-stage Space & Aerospace comparables or disclosed revenue for public-comp calibration.
